author: y4rr
content:
Fixed two flakes that surfaced under workspace-parallel test runs.

Flake 1 - CUDA stream-capture race in test_gpu_graph_pool:
- Three tests in test_gpu_graph_pool.rs do real CUDA stream captures (begin_capture..end_capture). Cargo runs tests within a binary in parallel; CUDAs stream-capture state machine has process-wide invariants that get violated when multiple captures interleave on the same context.
- Symptom: end_capture returned CUDA_ERROR_STREAM_CAPTURE_INVALIDATED when other capture work raced through the cudarc context.
- Fix: added a static Mutex<()> capture_lock() helper at the top of the test binary; the three real-capture tests acquire it for the duration of the begin..end critical section. The fourth capture test (pool_seal_blocks_begin_capture_with_pool) is exempt - it bails before invoking the CUDA API, so no real capture starts.
- Lock recovers from poisoning so a panic in one test does not cascade.

Flake 2 - test_dirichlet_concentrated per-sample tolerance:
- Surfaced after the CUDA fix as the next contention-sensitive flake, but the bug was actually statistical: with alpha=[100,100,100] the per-component std is sqrt(100*200/(300^2*301)) ~= 0.0272, and the test asserted each of 60 (3 components * 20 samples) was within 0.1 of 1/3 - that is ~3.7sigma, which fails ~0.4% of the time across 60 draws.
- Fix: switched to an empirical-mean assertion. With N_SAMPLES=200 and CLT, the means std is ~0.0272/sqrt(200) ~= 0.00193, so the 0.05 tolerance is ~26sigma - genuinely never fails for a correct sampler. Added an explicit positivity-and-le-1 simplex check per sample (hard constraints, not statistical).

Verification: 5 consecutive cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast runs - all 4599 passed, 0 failed.

Closes #602. (#603 was filed for the dirichlet fix and closed; both are subsumed under #602.)
